unity.title: C# and Editor Fundamentals
unity.description: This layer introduces the Unity editor interface and the C# language basics needed to script behavior. Mastering these foundations is essential before touching any game systems.

resources.label: Brackeys How to Make a Game

unity.challenge: Build a scene with a player cube that moves with keyboard input.

unity.title: Input and Movement
unity.description: This layer covers capturing player input and translating it into smooth character movement. Solid input handling is the backbone of any responsive game.
topics: Legacy input versus new Input System
unity.challenge: Add gamepad and keyboard support to the player with a follow camera.

unity.title: Physics and Collisions
unity.description: This layer explores the built-in physics engine for rigidbodies, colliders, and triggers. Understanding physics lets you create believable interactions and detection.
unity.challenge: Add jumping, gravity, and collectible pickups using triggers to the player.

unity.title: UI and Game State
unity.description: This layer teaches building menus, HUDs, and managing game state across scenes. Clear UI and state flow turn mechanics into an actual playable game.
unity.challenge: Add a main menu, score HUD, and game over screen to the project.

unity.title: Animation and State Machines
unity.description: This layer covers the Animator controller, blend trees, and event-driven animation. Animation brings characters and feedback to life and signals state to the player.
unity.challenge: Animate the player with idle, run, and jump states driven by movement.

unity.title: Audio and Visual Effects
unity.description: This layer adds sound, music, and particle effects to deepen immersion. Polished feedback through audio and effects makes mechanics feel satisfying.
unity.challenge: Add footstep sounds, background music, and a particle burst on pickups.

unity.title: Shader Graph and Materials
unity.description: This layer introduces node-based shaders and the rendering pipeline for custom visuals. Shaders let you craft a unique look without writing low-level code first.
unity.challenge: Create a dissolve shader for enemies and a glowing material for pickups.

unity.title: Data, Saving, and Scriptable Objects
unity.description: This layer covers persisting player progress and architecting data with Scriptable Objects. Clean data design keeps larger projects maintainable and decoupled.
unity.challenge: Add a save and load system that persists score and unlocked levels.

unity.title: AI and Navigation
unity.description: This layer builds enemy behavior using the NavMesh pathfinding and simple state machines. Believable AI raises challenge and makes the world feel reactive.
unity.challenge: Add patrolling enemies that chase the player when spotted using NavMesh.

unity.title: Optimization and Publishing
unity.description: This layer focuses on profiling, optimizing, and building for target platforms. Shipping a stable performant build is the final step that turns a project into a product.
unity.challenge: Profile and optimize the game, then produce a shippable Windows build.

unreal.title: Editor and Blueprint Basics
unreal.description: This layer introduces the Unreal editor and visual scripting with Blueprints. These fundamentals let you prototype gameplay before writing any C++.
unreal.challenge: Create a level with a Blueprint actor that rotates and reacts to overlap.

unreal.title: Player Input and Characters
unreal.description: This layer covers the Character class, possession, and the Enhanced Input system. Responsive character control is the core of any Unreal game.
unreal.challenge: Build a third person character with movement, jumping, and a follow camera.

unreal.title: C++ Gameplay Fundamentals
unreal.description: This layer transitions from Blueprints into Unreal C++ and the reflection system. C++ unlocks performance and control needed for serious gameplay code.
unreal.challenge: Rewrite the character base class in C++ and expose tunable values to Blueprints.

unreal.title: Collisions and Physics
unreal.description: This layer explores collision channels, traces, and the Chaos physics system. Reliable collision and physics drive combat, interaction, and destruction.
unreal.challenge: Add a projectile that traces hits and applies damage to physics actors.

unreal.title: UI with UMG
unreal.description: This layer teaches building menus and HUDs with the Unreal Motion Graphics system. Strong UI communicates state and ties mechanics into a full game loop.
unreal.challenge: Add a health bar, main menu, and game over widget to the project.

unreal.title: Animation and Skeletal Meshes
unreal.description: This layer covers animation blueprints, blend spaces, and state machines for characters. Quality animation sells movement and gives combat its impact.
unreal.challenge: Set up a locomotion state machine and an attack montage for the character.

unreal.title: Materials and Nanite
unreal.description: This layer dives into the material editor plus Nanite geometry and Lumen lighting. Modern rendering features deliver high fidelity scenes efficiently.
unreal.challenge: Build a Nanite environment with Lumen lighting and a custom material instance.

unreal.title: Gameplay Ability System
unreal.description: This layer introduces the Gameplay Ability System for abilities, attributes, and effects. GAS provides a scalable framework for complex combat and RPG mechanics.
unreal.challenge: Implement a dash ability and a health attribute using the ability system.

unreal.title: Multiplayer and Replication
unreal.description: This layer covers the Unreal networking model, replication, and RPCs. Networked play is a defining strength of Unreal for multiplayer titles.
unreal.challenge: Make the character and projectile replicate correctly in a listen server session.

unreal.title: Packaging and Optimization
unreal.description: This layer focuses on profiling with Unreal Insights and packaging shippable builds. Optimizing and packaging is the last mile that delivers a playable product.
unreal.challenge: Profile the game with Insights and produce an optimized packaged build.

web-game.title: JavaScript and Canvas Basics
web-game.description: This layer establishes JavaScript fundamentals and drawing to the HTML5 canvas. These basics underpin every browser-based game you will build.
web-game.challenge: Draw a player sprite on canvas that moves with arrow keys in a game loop.

web-game.title: Phaser Game Framework
web-game.description: This layer introduces Phaser for scenes, sprites, and arcade physics. A framework speeds up development and handles common 2D game plumbing.
web-game.challenge: Build a 2D platformer prototype with a tilemap level and arcade physics.

web-game.title: Sprites, Animation, and Audio
web-game.description: This layer covers sprite sheets, animations, and sound for richer 2D games. Animation and audio give a web game its feel and feedback.
web-game.challenge: Add animated walk and jump cycles plus sound effects to the platformer.

web-game.title: Game State and UI
web-game.description: This layer manages score, lives, and menus across multiple scenes. Clear UI and state flow turn a prototype into a complete game loop.
web-game.challenge: Add a menu, score HUD, and high score saving to the platformer.

web-game.title: WebGL and 3D with Three.js
web-game.description: This layer introduces 3D rendering in the browser using Three.js over WebGL. Moving to 3D opens a new dimension of web game possibilities.
web-game.challenge: Build a simple 3D scene with a controllable model and a follow camera.

web-game.title: 3D Physics and Interaction
web-game.description: This layer adds a physics engine and raycasting for 3D interaction. Physics and picking make 3D web games tangible and playable.
web-game.challenge: Add gravity, collisions, and clickable objects to the 3D scene.

web-game.title: Shaders and Visual Effects
web-game.description: This layer introduces GLSL shaders and post processing for custom visuals. Shaders give web games a distinct and polished look.
web-game.challenge: Write a custom shader material and add a bloom post processing effect.

web-game.title: Networking and Multiplayer
web-game.description: This layer connects browsers to a server for real-time multiplayer. Networking enables shared sessions and competitive web games.
web-game.challenge: Add real-time multiplayer movement to a game using WebSockets.

web-game.title: Asset Pipeline and Tooling
web-game.description: This layer covers bundling, asset loading, and build tooling for web games. A solid pipeline keeps load times low and the project maintainable.
web-game.challenge: Set up a Vite build with optimized asset loading for the game project.

web-game.title: Optimization and Deployment
web-game.description: This layer focuses on performance profiling and deploying a web game to the public. Shipping a fast loading playable build completes the journey.
web-game.challenge: Profile, optimize, and deploy the finished game to itch.io or a host.

game-designer.title: Design Fundamentals
game-designer.description: This layer introduces core design vocabulary, player motivation, and the role of a designer. A shared foundation in theory frames every decision that follows.
game-designer.challenge: Write a one page concept defining the core loop and pillars of a small game.

game-designer.title: The Game Design Document
game-designer.description: This layer teaches structuring a clear and living game design document. A good GDD aligns the team and turns ideas into actionable plans.
topics: One page and full design docs
game-designer.challenge: Expand the concept into a structured GDD with scope and feature list.

game-designer.title: Systems and Mechanics
game-designer.description: This layer explores designing interlocking systems and the rules that govern them. Strong systems thinking creates depth and emergent play.
game-designer.challenge: Design a core mechanic and a supporting progression system for your game.

game-designer.title: Level and Encounter Design
game-designer.description: This layer covers pacing, layout, and guiding players through space. Level design shapes the moment to moment experience of a game.
game-designer.challenge: Block out a tutorial level that teaches your core mechanic without text.

game-designer.title: Prototyping in Unity
game-designer.description: This layer gets designers building playable prototypes in Unity. Prototyping turns paper ideas into testable interactive experiences.
game-designer.challenge: Build a gray box playable prototype of your core mechanic in Unity.

game-designer.title: Narrative and Branching
game-designer.description: This layer covers story structure and branching narrative using Twine. Narrative design weaves meaning and choice into the player journey.
game-designer.challenge: Create a short branching narrative for your game world in Twine.

game-designer.title: Balancing and Economy
game-designer.description: This layer teaches numerical balancing of stats, costs, and rewards. Balance keeps a game fair, challenging, and satisfying over time.
game-designer.challenge: Build a spreadsheet that balances your game economy and progression.

game-designer.title: UX and Interface Design
game-designer.description: This layer covers usability, menus, and clear feedback for players. Good UX removes friction so players engage with the design directly.
game-designer.challenge: Design HUD and menu wireframes for your prototype in Figma.

game-designer.title: Playtesting and Iteration
game-designer.description: This layer focuses on running playtests and iterating from feedback. Disciplined testing exposes problems no amount of theory can predict.
game-designer.challenge: Run a structured playtest of your prototype and document changes to make.

game-designer.title: Pitching and Shipping
game-designer.description: This layer covers pitching, vertical slices, and preparing a design for release. Communicating and finalizing the vision is what gets a game made and shipped.
game-designer.challenge: Produce a polished pitch deck and vertical slice plan for your game.

godot.title: Godot and GDScript Basics
godot.description: This layer introduces the Godot editor and the GDScript language. These fundamentals let you build and script nodes for any game.
godot.challenge: Build a scene with a sprite node that moves using keyboard input.

godot.title: Nodes, Scenes, and Signals
godot.description: This layer explores composing reusable scenes and connecting them with signals. The node and signal model is the heart of Godot architecture.
godot.challenge: Create reusable enemy and coin scenes that emit signals when collected.

godot.title: 2D Physics and Movement
godot.description: This layer covers the 2D physics bodies and building a character controller. Reliable movement and collision drive a satisfying platformer.
godot.challenge: Build a platformer character with gravity, jumping, and coin pickups.

godot.title: UI and Game Flow
godot.description: This layer teaches building menus and HUDs using Control nodes. UI and scene switching turn mechanics into a full game loop.
godot.challenge: Add a main menu, score HUD, and game over screen to the platformer.

godot.title: Animation and Tweens
godot.description: This layer covers AnimationPlayer, AnimatedSprite, and tweening for motion. Animation adds polish and clear feedback to gameplay.
godot.challenge: Animate the character with idle run and jump states and tweened pickups.

godot.title: Audio and Particles
godot.description: This layer adds sound, music, and particle effects for immersion. Audio and effects make interactions feel responsive and alive.
godot.challenge: Add jump and pickup sounds, music, and a particle burst to the game.

godot.title: Shaders in Godot
godot.description: This layer introduces the Godot shading language for custom 2D effects. Shaders let you craft a distinctive visual identity.
godot.challenge: Write a hit flash and outline shader for enemies in the game.

godot.title: C# in Godot and Architecture
godot.description: This layer adds C# scripting and clean project architecture in Godot. C# brings strong typing and tooling for larger codebases.
godot.challenge: Rewrite a core system in C# and add a save and load feature.

godot.title: Enemy AI and Levels
godot.description: This layer builds enemy AI with navigation and assembles full levels. Reactive enemies and varied levels make a complete experience.
godot.challenge: Add patrolling and chasing enemies and build a multi-level world.

godot.title: Exporting and Optimization
godot.description: This layer covers profiling, optimizing, and exporting to platforms. Producing a shippable build is the final step to release.
godot.challenge: Profile and optimize the game then export a build for desktop and web.

game-ai.title: AI Foundations
game-ai.description: This layer introduces game AI concepts and the math behind agent behavior. A grounding in fundamentals prepares you for advanced techniques.
game-ai.challenge: Implement a simple agent that moves toward a target each frame.

game-ai.title: Finite State Machines
game-ai.description: This layer covers finite state machines for structuring agent behavior. FSMs are the most common and approachable AI architecture.
game-ai.challenge: Build an enemy with idle patrol and chase states using an FSM.

game-ai.title: Pathfinding and Navigation
game-ai.description: This layer teaches grid and navmesh pathfinding with the A star algorithm. Pathfinding lets agents move intelligently through the world.
game-ai.challenge: Implement A star pathfinding so enemies navigate around obstacles to the player.

game-ai.title: Steering Behaviors
game-ai.description: This layer covers steering behaviors like seek, flee, and flocking. Steering produces smooth lifelike movement that pathfinding alone lacks.
game-ai.challenge: Add flocking and obstacle avoidance to a group of patrolling enemies.

game-ai.title: Behavior Trees
game-ai.description: This layer introduces behavior trees as a scalable decision architecture. Behavior trees handle complex AI more cleanly than large state machines.

resources.label: Behavior Trees in Robotics and AI

game-ai.challenge: Replace the enemy FSM with a behavior tree controlling patrol and combat.

game-ai.title: Perception and Sensing
game-ai.description: This layer covers giving agents sight, hearing, and memory of the world. Perception systems make AI react believably to the player.
topics: Vision cones and line of sight
game-ai.challenge: Add a vision cone and sound detection so enemies investigate the player.

game-ai.title: GOAP and Planning
game-ai.description: This layer introduces Goal Oriented Action Planning for flexible behavior. Planning lets agents choose actions dynamically to reach goals.
game-ai.challenge: Implement a GOAP agent that plans actions to hunt and ambush the player.

game-ai.title: Utility AI and Tactics
game-ai.description: This layer covers utility based decision making and tactical reasoning. Utility AI scores options for nuanced and adaptive behavior.
game-ai.challenge: Add utility scoring so enemies choose between attacking fleeing and taking cover.

game-ai.title: Group and Coordinated AI
game-ai.description: This layer teaches coordinating multiple agents for squad and crowd behavior. Coordinated AI creates emergent and memorable encounters.
game-ai.challenge: Coordinate a squad of enemies to flank and surround the player.

game-ai.title: Profiling and Shipping AI
game-ai.description: This layer focuses on profiling, optimizing, and debugging AI at scale. Performant and tunable AI is what ships in a real game.
game-ai.challenge: Profile and time slice the AI then add debug visualization for a shippable build.

shader-dev.title: Graphics and Math Foundations
shader-dev.description: This layer covers the rendering pipeline and the math shaders rely on. A solid foundation in vectors and matrices is essential for graphics work.
shader-dev.challenge: Write a fragment shader that outputs a gradient based on screen coordinates.

shader-dev.title: Fragment Shader Fundamentals
shader-dev.description: This layer dives into fragment shaders, uniforms, and procedural color. Fragment shaders are where most visual magic happens.
shader-dev.challenge: Create an animated procedural pattern using UVs and time in a fragment shader.

shader-dev.title: Texturing and Sampling
shader-dev.description: This layer covers sampling textures, blending, and UV manipulation. Texturing brings detail and material variety to surfaces.
shader-dev.challenge: Build a material that blends two textures and applies a normal map.

shader-dev.title: Lighting Models
shader-dev.description: This layer teaches lighting from diffuse and specular to physically based rendering. Lighting determines how believable a surface appears.
shader-dev.challenge: Implement a PBR lit material with metalness and roughness inputs.

shader-dev.title: Unity URP Shaders
shader-dev.description: This layer covers writing shaders for the Universal Render Pipeline in Unity. URP is a common target for stylized and performant game visuals.
shader-dev.challenge: Write a custom URP lit shader with a stylized toon lighting ramp.

shader-dev.title: Vertex Shaders and Deformation
shader-dev.description: This layer explores vertex shaders for animation and mesh deformation. Vertex work enables waves, wind, and dynamic geometry effects.
shader-dev.challenge: Create a wind animated foliage shader using vertex displacement.

shader-dev.title: Post Processing Effects
shader-dev.description: This layer covers full screen post processing like bloom and color grading. Post effects unify and elevate the overall look of a scene.
shader-dev.challenge: Build a post process stack with bloom color grading and edge outlines.

shader-dev.title: Compute and GPU Programming
shader-dev.description: This layer introduces compute shaders for general GPU computation. Compute unlocks particles, simulations, and heavy parallel work.
shader-dev.challenge: Implement a GPU particle system driven by a compute shader.

shader-dev.title: Vulkan and Modern APIs
shader-dev.description: This layer covers the explicit Vulkan API and modern graphics pipelines. Low level APIs give maximum control over rendering performance.
shader-dev.challenge: Render a textured lit triangle with a custom SPIR-V shader in Vulkan.

shader-dev.title: Optimization and Profiling
shader-dev.description: This layer focuses on profiling and optimizing shaders for shipping. Efficient shaders are what allow rich visuals to run at target frame rates.
shader-dev.challenge: Profile your shaders with RenderDoc and optimize them for a target platform.

game-backend.title: Node.js and Server Basics
game-backend.description: This layer establishes Node.js and HTTP server fundamentals for game services. Backend basics are the foundation for all online game features.
game-backend.challenge: Build a REST API that creates and fetches player profiles.

game-backend.title: Real-Time with WebSockets
game-backend.description: This layer covers real-time bidirectional communication using WebSockets. Real-time messaging is essential for multiplayer game backends.
game-backend.challenge: Build a chat and lobby server where players join rooms in real time.

game-backend.title: Authentication and Accounts
game-backend.description: This layer teaches secure player authentication and account management. Trustworthy auth protects player identity and progress.
game-backend.challenge: Add account registration and JWT login to the game backend.

game-backend.title: Databases and Persistence
game-backend.description: This layer covers storing player data in SQL and NoSQL databases. Reliable persistence keeps progress and game state safe.
game-backend.challenge: Persist player profiles inventory and stats to a database.

game-backend.title: Caching with Redis
game-backend.description: This layer introduces Redis for caching, sessions, and fast shared state. In memory data stores power leaderboards and matchmaking speed.
game-backend.challenge: Build a real-time leaderboard using Redis sorted sets.

game-backend.title: Game State and Authority
game-backend.description: This layer covers authoritative server state and synchronization. Server authority prevents cheating and keeps players in sync.
game-backend.challenge: Build an authoritative server loop that syncs player positions to clients.

game-backend.title: Matchmaking and Lobbies
game-backend.description: This layer teaches building matchmaking, lobbies, and party systems. Matchmaking pairs players fairly and forms the social layer.
game-backend.challenge: Add a matchmaking queue that groups players into game sessions.

game-backend.title: Managed Services with PlayFab
game-backend.description: This layer covers managed backend platforms like PlayFab for live games. Managed services accelerate features like economy and analytics.
game-backend.challenge: Integrate PlayFab for player accounts a virtual economy and leaderboards.

game-backend.title: Scaling and Infrastructure
game-backend.description: This layer covers containerization, load balancing, and horizontal scaling. Scalable infrastructure keeps a game online under heavy load.
game-backend.challenge: Containerize the backend and run multiple game server instances behind a balancer.

game-backend.title: Security, Monitoring, and Launch
game-backend.description: This layer focuses on hardening, monitoring, and launching the backend. Observability and security are what keep a live service healthy.
game-backend.challenge: Add monitoring rate limiting and logging then deploy the backend to production.

xr-dev.title: XR Foundations
xr-dev.description: This layer introduces XR concepts, hardware, and the Unity XR toolkit. Understanding the landscape prepares you to build for AR and VR.
xr-dev.challenge: Set up an XR project and display a tracked headset view in the editor.

xr-dev.title: VR Interaction Basics
xr-dev.description: This layer covers controllers, grabbing, and interaction in VR. Natural interaction is what makes virtual reality feel real.
xr-dev.challenge: Build a VR scene where you can grab throw and teleport between objects.

xr-dev.title: Locomotion and Comfort
xr-dev.description: This layer covers movement schemes and reducing motion sickness in VR. Comfortable locomotion keeps players immersed and well.
xr-dev.challenge: Implement multiple locomotion options with a comfort vignette in your VR scene.

xr-dev.title: AR with ARKit and ARFoundation
xr-dev.description: This layer introduces mobile AR using ARFoundation, ARKit, and ARCore. AR places virtual content into the real world through the camera.
xr-dev.challenge: Build an AR app that detects surfaces and places a virtual model on them.

xr-dev.title: Hand and Body Tracking
xr-dev.description: This layer covers hand tracking, gestures, and body input. Hands free interaction deepens presence and accessibility.
xr-dev.challenge: Add hand tracking with pinch gestures to interact with objects in VR.

xr-dev.title: Spatial UI and Audio
xr-dev.description: This layer covers designing UI and audio that live in 3D space. Spatial UI and sound are key to comfortable and immersive XR.
xr-dev.challenge: Build a spatial menu with pointer interaction and spatialized audio cues.

xr-dev.title: WebXR and Cross Platform
xr-dev.description: This layer introduces browser based XR with the WebXR API. WebXR reaches users without app installs across many devices.
xr-dev.challenge: Build a WebXR scene viewable in a browser based VR headset session.

xr-dev.title: Multiplayer and Social XR
xr-dev.description: This layer covers shared XR spaces with avatars and voice. Social presence transforms XR into a connected experience.
xr-dev.challenge: Build a shared VR room where two networked avatars can interact together.

xr-dev.title: Performance and Optimization
xr-dev.description: This layer focuses on hitting strict XR frame rate and rendering budgets. Performance is critical for comfort and avoiding sickness in XR.
xr-dev.challenge: Profile and optimize your XR app to hold the target frame rate on a headset.

xr-dev.title: Publishing XR Apps
xr-dev.description: This layer covers building, testing, and publishing to XR stores. Shipping through store review is the final step for an XR title.
xr-dev.challenge: Produce a signed XR build and prepare a store submission package.

gameplay-engineer.title: Programming Foundations
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer solidifies core programming and the languages of game engines. Strong fundamentals are the bedrock of gameplay engineering.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Build a small console game loop that updates and renders entities each tick.

gameplay-engineer.title: Game Loops and Architecture
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer covers the core game loop and common architectural patterns. Clean architecture keeps gameplay code scalable and testable.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Build a component based entity system with an event driven game loop.

gameplay-engineer.title: Character Controllers
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer focuses on building responsive character movement and controllers. The character controller is the player primary connection to the game.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Build a polished character controller with jumping coyote time and input buffering.

gameplay-engineer.title: Combat and Abilities
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer covers building combat systems, abilities, and damage. Combat systems are a core expression of gameplay engineering.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Implement a melee and ranged combat system with cooldowns and damage.

gameplay-engineer.title: Animation Programming
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer covers driving animation from gameplay code and state machines. Animation programming ties player intent to visible motion.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Drive a full locomotion and attack animation set from gameplay state.

gameplay-engineer.title: Gameplay Systems and Data
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer covers data driven systems for inventory, quests, and progression. Data driven design lets designers build content without code changes.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Build a data driven inventory and simple quest system with saving.

gameplay-engineer.title: Multiplayer Gameplay
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer covers replicating gameplay and handling networked play. Networked gameplay is a demanding and valuable engineering skill.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Replicate the character and combat system for two networked players.

gameplay-engineer.title: Tools and Workflow
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer covers building editor tools and debugging aids for the team. Good tooling multiplies the productivity of an entire studio.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Build an editor tool and debug overlay for tuning your gameplay systems.

gameplay-engineer.title: Performance and Memory
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer covers profiling, optimizing, and managing memory in gameplay code. Performance work ensures gameplay runs smoothly at scale.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Profile and optimize your gameplay systems to remove frame time spikes.

gameplay-engineer.title: Polish and Shipping
gameplay-engineer.description: This layer focuses on game feel polish, bug fixing, and shipping. The final pass is what separates a prototype from a finished game.
gameplay-engineer.challenge: Add game feel polish fix priority bugs and produce a shippable build.

tools-dev.title: Tools Programming Foundations
tools-dev.description: This layer introduces the mindset and languages of game tools development. Tools engineers build the software that lets teams make games faster.
tools-dev.challenge: Write a command line script that batch renames and organizes asset files.

tools-dev.title: Unity Editor Scripting
tools-dev.description: This layer covers extending the Unity editor with custom inspectors and windows. Editor scripting is the core of tools work inside Unity.
tools-dev.challenge: Build a custom inspector and editor window for configuring a game component.

tools-dev.title: Asset Pipelines and Importers
tools-dev.description: This layer covers automating asset import, processing, and validation. Reliable pipelines keep large asset libraries consistent and clean.
tools-dev.challenge: Build an import pipeline that validates and configures textures automatically.

tools-dev.title: Standalone Tools with Qt
tools-dev.description: This layer covers building desktop tools with Qt outside the engine. Standalone tools serve pipelines that span multiple applications.
tools-dev.challenge: Build a standalone Qt tool that previews and batch converts asset files.

tools-dev.title: Data Driven Tooling
tools-dev.description: This layer covers building data formats, editors, and serialization for content. Data driven tools empower designers to author without programmers.
tools-dev.challenge: Build a data editor that imports a spreadsheet into validated game data.

tools-dev.title: Level and Content Editors
tools-dev.description: This layer covers building in editor level and content authoring tools. Custom editors let designers craft content efficiently and safely.
tools-dev.challenge: Build a scene tool for painting and placing props with undo support.

tools-dev.title: Automation and Build Tools
tools-dev.description: This layer covers automating builds, tests, and repetitive tasks. Automation removes toil and keeps builds reproducible and reliable.
tools-dev.challenge: Set up an automated build pipeline that produces and reports a game build.

tools-dev.title: Debugging and Profiling Tools
tools-dev.description: This layer covers building debug overlays, profilers, and diagnostic tools. Diagnostic tools help the whole team find and fix problems fast.
tools-dev.challenge: Build an in game debug console and overlay with custom profiler markers.

tools-dev.title: Pipeline Integration
tools-dev.description: This layer covers integrating tools across DCC apps, source control, and engines. Connected pipelines move content smoothly from artist to game.
tools-dev.challenge: Build a bridge that exports assets from a DCC tool into the engine automatically.

tools-dev.title: Distribution and Maintenance
tools-dev.description: This layer covers packaging, versioning, and maintaining tools for a team. Well maintained tools stay useful and trusted across a studio.
tools-dev.challenge: Package your tools as a versioned distributable with documentation for the team.
